definition: I2S synthesizes ELF header field values (magic, class, section-header offset/count) so that section_count() > 1, enabling a loop guard to fire and the loop body to execute.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2S substitutes ELF magic and class/section-count header operands from the CmpLog buffer into matching input offsets, raising the decoded section_count above 1 so the loop guard becomes true and ReadSection executes.
signature.one_line: I2S plants header bytes to raise section_count above 1, enabling the loop body

analysis.summary_one_line: A valid ELF header whose e_shnum (section_count) decodes to >=2, so the section-iteration loop body executes

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The input must parse as an ELF whose header fields (magic 7f 45 4c 46, class/endianness/version bytes at 0x04-0x07, and the section-header-count field) decode to a section_count() value of 2 or more, so the loop guard i < elf.section_count() is true on the first iteration (i=1).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The cmplog winner seeds carry well-formed ELF identification bytes (e.g. 7f 45 4c 46 02 02 03 00 ... and 7f 45 4c 46 02 01 ...) plus header fields that make section_count() large: in the W role section_count() is invoked 286000 times and the loop guard at line 856 takes the true side (T=57200), whereas the loser does not, so the loop body and ReadSection run only for the winner.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser seeds keep section_count() effectively at <=1 so the line-856 guard never takes the true branch (L role at line 856 shows T=0 F=29); section_count() fires only 222 times in L vs 286000 in W, and ReadSection (line 536) fires 0 times in L, meaning the loop body is never entered.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the per-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes one CMP operand into input offsets matching the other operand, letting it plant the exact header byte values (magic/class/section-count) that the ELF parser compares against, so the decoded section_count() crosses the loop bound. Naive has only blind havoc with no CMP introspection and cannot reliably hit the multi-byte header constraints.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the section-iteration loop guard whose true side is globally blocked.
The true (loop-entering) side is the globally blocked side.
cmplog resolves the branch in all 10 trials while naive is blocked in all 10, a single I2S-delta decisive pair.
In the winner role the line-856 guard takes the true side while in the loser role it never does.

ReadSection (the loop body) executes only in the winner role, confirming the loser never enters the loop.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic ELF-parsing harness whose loop bound is set by a section-count header field where naive resolves the loop-entry branch as fast as cmplog (no significant cmplog>naive divergence), showing the multi-byte header constraint is not what gates the loop.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2S reads section-count and magic-field CMP operands from the post-execution buffer and plants them back into ELF header offsets, synthesizing a section_count exceeding 1 that naive's blind havoc never constructs.
signature.one_line: I2S manufactures a valid section-count header so the loop guard evaluates true

analysis.summary_one_line: ELF header bytes that parse to a section-header table with section_count() >= 2, so the loop body is entered.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A valid ELF header at offset 0x00 (magic 7f 45 4c 46 plus class/data/version fields) whose section-table fields (e_shnum / e_shoff and related header words at offsets ~0x04-0x2f) parse such that elf.section_count() returns a value >= 2, making the loop condition i < section_count() true for i starting at 1.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry the ELF magic and structured header bytes with non-0x20 values in the class/data/version and section-count region (e.g. 02 02 03 at offsets 0x04-0x06, concentrated low integer values at 0x07-0x2f), so the parsed header yields section_count() of 286000 entry hits in W and ReadSection fires; the true side of branch 991 is taken (W resolves 10/10).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds fill the section-table header region with 0x20 (space) padding runs (offsets 0x0a-0x1c are dominated by 20 20 20 ... in L), so the parsed header gives section_count()==0/1; section_count() entry-line is hit only 222 times in L vs 286000 in W and the loop body (ReadSection, line 993) is never entered, so the false side is taken (L blocks 10/10).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads CMP operands from the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer (here the integer comparison i < section_count() and the magic/header field compares inside the ELF struct reader) and substitutes the recorded comparand back into the matching input offsets, manufacturing a header whose parsed section_count exceeds 1; naive lacks I2S and must hit the multi-byte header layout by blind havoc, which it never does in 10 trials.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked branch is the loop guard whose true side enters the per-section loop body, requiring section_count() greater than the start index 1.
cmplog resolves the branch 10/10 while naive blocks 10/10, a single I2S-delta decisive pair.
section_count() and ReadSection fire heavily in the winner and almost never in the loser, showing the loop body is entered only in W.
Winner seeds carry a structured ELF header (magic plus class/data/version 02 02 03) rather than padding.
Loser seeds fill the section-table header region with 0x20 space padding runs.
Byte-diff shows winner header bytes are low concentrated integers while loser bytes are 0x20 space runs at the section-count region offsets.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which scans the input for a recorded CMP operand and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S; it relies on havoc plus token mutation only.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic ELF-parsing harness gating on section_count()>1 where cmplog and naive resolve the loop-body branch at statistically indistinguishable rates, showing I2S substitution gives no advantage in reaching section_count>=2.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The exact header field (e_shnum vs e_shoff or a derived count) that section_count() reads is inferred from source/byte-diff, not directly confirmed, so the precise input offset that must be substituted is uncertain.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2S reads section-table header field CMP operands and substitutes them at matching offsets, producing a self-consistent section_count above 1 and making the loop condition true; lineage dash-row confirms I2S ancestry.
signature.one_line: I2S synthesizes consistent section-table bytes so section_count exceeds 1 for the loop

analysis.summary_one_line: ELF header section-table fields (e_shoff/e_shnum/e_shentsize region) must encode a valid section_count() greater than 1 to enter the loop body.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The ELF header section-table descriptor bytes (section-header offset / count / entry-size, derived from header fields in the 0x05-0x3f region after the 7f454c46 magic) must form a structurally consistent table so elf.section_count() returns a value greater than 1, making the loop condition i < elf.section_count() true at least once.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner cmplog seeds carry structured header fields (e.g. class/data bytes 02 02 03 at 0x04-0x06 and structured table descriptors around 0x17-0x1f such as 21(!)/98/38/10) and DO take the true side: the blocker branch shows T=40 F=24 in W versus T=0 in L, and ReadSection/Section::header() fire 287000 times in W vs 0 in L, proving section_count()>1 and the loop body executing.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser naive seeds leave the section-table region filler/uniform (offsets 0x17-0x1f are 20( ) padding or ff/00 runs, e.g. 0x18 is 20( )x10 and 0x1d-0x1f are ff/00 in L), so section_count() collapses to <=1; the loop condition is always false (L1033 T=0 F=57) and section_count() is entered only 222 times vs 286000 in W.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the per-execution CmpLogObserver buffer for the integer comparisons that validate the section-table header fields, locates the matching operand byte sequence in the input, and substitutes the expected operand at those header offsets, synthesizing a self-consistent section count that naive's blind havoc cannot land on; lineage shows I2S-floor dash rows in the winner's ancestor chain.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is a loop guard whose true side requires elf.section_count() to exceed 1.
The globally blocked side is the true (loop-entering) branch.
Winner takes the loop's true side while loser never does.
The loop body fires ReadSection and Section::header() hundreds of thousands of times in W and never in L, confirming section_count()>1 only for the winner.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2S substitutes logged CMP operands for ELF section-header offset and count fields into the input, yielding a section_count above 1 so the DWARF-section loop guard passes and the loop body executes.
signature.one_line: I2S fills ELF section-header fields to produce section_count greater than 1

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must be a 64-bit ELF whose header e_shnum/e_shoff fields yield section_count() > 1 so the loop guard's true side fires

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A valid 64-bit ELF header (magic 7f 45 4c 46, ELFCLASS64 = 0x02 at offset 0x04) whose section-header-table fields (e_shoff at 0x28 and e_shnum near 0x3c) parse to a section_count() strictly greater than 1, so the loop guard i < elf.section_count() takes its true side and the body at lines 1194-1239 executes.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner cmplog seeds set offset 0x04 to 0x02 (ELFCLASS64) and carry non-trivial header offset/count fields, so ElfFile::section_count() returns ~286000 hits in W vs ~230 in L and the blocker line reports T=16 in W versus T=0 in L; the loop body lines 1194-1239 are W-only.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser naive seeds use offset 0x04 = 0x01 (ELFCLASS32) with mostly-zeroed or 0x20-padded section-table fields, so section_count() resolves to 0/1, the guard is always false (L1193 shows T=0 F=20), the loop body never runs, and ElfFile::section_count() is hit only ~230 times in L.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes one logged CMP operand at input offsets matching the other, letting it splice the precise section-count/offset comparand values into the header so the loop guard comparison passes; naive has only blind havoc and never solves the multi-field header constraint.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is a loop guard comparing the index against the ELF section count, with the true side globally blocked.
Winner seeds set offset 0x04 to 0x02 (ELFCLASS64) while loser seeds use 0x01 (ELFCLASS32).
The loop body executes only in the winner role; the body lines 1194-1239 are marked [W].
section_count() is invoked ~286000 times in the winner versus ~230 in the loser, showing the count is non-zero only for winners.
The blocker line takes its true side in the winner (T=16) but never in the loser (T=0).

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The exact offsets that feed section_count() (e_shoff/e_shnum) are inferred from ELF layout plus diffuse PARTIAL byte-diff signal rather than a single concentrated I2S-substituted offset, so the precise comparand splice point is uncertain.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace intercepts integer CMPs on ELF header fields (e_shoff/e_shnum/e_shentsize) and substitutes correlated section-table values into input bytes, raising section_count() above 1 to open the loop.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A valid ELF header (7f 45 4c 46 magic) whose e_shoff / e_shnum / e_shentsize section-header-table fields parse to a section count greater than 1, so the loop condition i < elf.section_count() is true on the first iteration and the loop body at lines 1280-1291 executes.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The cmplog winner seeds carry the ELF magic and structured header bytes that drive section_count() to fire 286000 times in W vs 230 in L, and the blocker true side is taken (T=228000) only in W; ReadSection and Section::header() run 287000 times in W and 0 in L, showing the loop body actually iterates over real sections.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser seeds keep header fields that make section_count() evaluate to <=1, so the loop condition i<section_count() is immediately false: the blocker shows T=0 F=120 in L (loop never enters), Section::header()/ReadSection have 0 L hits, and instead the segment path (Segment::header() 2130000 L hits, GetSegmentName 427000 L hits) is exercised.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the per-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the comparand operands of the integer CMPs that the StructReader/section-count parsing performs on the header bytes, splicing the matching section-table field values into the input so section_count() crosses 1; naive has only havoc/token mutators and no CMP introspection, so it cannot reliably synthesize the correlated header fields.
